rationale.R1.7: Only one explicit citation appears (Eyring et al., 2016); several other claims rely on implicit attribution without references.
rationale.RX.1: The output does not disclose uncertainty for any clearly out-of-reach claims, and all output is presented as equally confident.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested; the artifact discusses various topics beyond the agreed scope (e.g., generative agents, climate adaptation).
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content: the 20–40% adaptation-gap figure is an unsupported quantitative claim.
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability; several claims rely on implicit attribution without references.
